entries.title: LatAm's Self-Taught Cyber Talent Overlooked Amid Cyberattack Glut
entries.summary: A newly released study exclusively shared with Dark Reading details the unique circumstances that make up Latin America's labor pool, and why organizations may want to expand their talent search.

entries.title: Cyberattacks Intensify Pressure on Latin American Governments
entries.summary: Cyber threats across Latin America are increasingly targeting government systems, from disruptive attacks in Puerto Rico to a surge of probes against Colombia’s health sector.

entries.title: Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Commoditizes ClickFix Attacks
entries.summary: A new service on the cybercrime market provides automated capabilities to create persistent information-stealing social engineering attacks.

entries.title: Are We Training AI Too Late?
entries.summary: Ask the Expert: Cybersecurity teams need to expand their field of view to include new, unique threat sources, rather than relying on past, proven threat actors.

entries.title: The Forgotten Endpoint: Security Risks of Dormant Devices
entries.summary: Technology Talk: That forgotten notebook holds plenty of secrets to enterprise access.

entries.title: Axios NPM Package Compromised in Precision Attack
entries.summary: The NPM package for Axios, a popular JavaScript HTTP client library, was briefly compromised this week, possibly by North Korean threat actors.

entries.title: Google's Vertex AI Is Over-Privileged. That's a Problem
entries.summary: Palo Alto Networks researchers show how attackers could exploit AI agents on Google's Vertex AI to steal data and break into restricted cloud infrastructure.

entries.title: TeamPCP Breaches Cloud, SaaS Instances With Stolen Credentials
entries.summary: The threat group's shift to speedy attacks on AWS, Azure, and SaaS instances shows organizations need to respond quickly to compromised credentials.

entries.title: AI and Quantum Are Forcing a Rethink of Digital Trust
entries.summary: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, DigiCert CEO Amit Sinha explains how AI-driven identities and quantum threats are reshaping the foundations of digital trust.

entries.title: Black Hat USA
Iran Deploys 'Pseudo-Ransomware,' Revives Pay2Key Operations
entries.summary: Iranian APTs are blurring the lines between state-sponsored and cybercriminal activities to target high-impact US organizations.

entries.title: AI-Powered 'DeepLoad' Malware Steals Credentials, Evades Detection
entries.summary: The massive amount of junk code that hides the malware's logic from security scans was almost certainly generated by AI, researchers say.

entries.title: AI-Driven Code Surge Is Forcing a Rethink of AppSec
entries.summary: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, Black Duck CEO Jason Schmitt explains how AI is reshaping application security and why it must evolve to keep pace.

entries.title: F5 BIG-IP Vulnerability Reclassified as RCE, Under Exploitation
entries.summary: CVE-2025-53521 was initially disclosed in October as a high-severity denial-of-service (DoS) flaw, but new information has revealed the bug is actually much more dangerous.

entries.title: Manufacturing & Healthcare Share Struggles with Passwords
entries.summary: The two key economic sectors struggle with security for a reason: Many insiders view access management as a roadblock, while attackers see it as a way in.

entries.title: Storm Brews Over Critical, No-Click Telegram Flaw
entries.summary: The vulnerability, which is allegedly triggered by a corrupted sticker in the messaging app, received a 9.8 CVSS score, but Telegram denies it exists.

entries.title: China Upgrades the Backdoor It Uses to Spy on Telcos Globally
entries.summary: Chinese APT Red Menshen's super-advanced BPFdoor malware defeats traditional cybersecurity protections. All telcos can do, really, is try hunting it down.

entries.title: Wartime Usage of Compromised IP Cameras Highlight Their Danger
entries.summary: The list of countries exploiting Internet-connected cameras to give them eyes inside their adversaries' borders continues to expand. What should companies look out for?

entries.title: Infrastructure Attacks With Physical Consequences Down 25%
entries.summary: Operational technology (OT) at industrial and critical infrastructure sites seem to have been benefitting from a lull in ransomware, and hackers' relative ignorance of OT systems.

entries.title: Google Sets 2029 Deadline for Quantum-Safe Cryptography
entries.summary: The post-quantum future may be coming sooner than you think, as Google plans to have PQC migration in place by 2029.

entries.title: Coruna, DarkSword & Democratizing Nation-State Exploit Kits
entries.summary: Nation-state malware is being sold on the Dark Web and leaked to GitHub; and ordinary organizations might not stand much of a chance of defending themselves.

entries.title: Is the FCC's Router Ban the Wrong Fix?
entries.summary: The agency put foreign-made consumer routers on its list of prohibited communications devices, but the ban could create more problems down the road.

entries.title: Automotive Cybersecurity Threats Grow in Era of Connected, Autonomous Vehicles
entries.summary: More than a decade since the 2015 Jeep hack, the cybersecurity of vehicles remains of the utmost importance.

entries.title: Critical Flaw in Langflow AI Platform Under Attack
entries.summary: Threats actors pounced on the code injection vulnerability within hours of its disclosure, demonstrating that organizations have little time to address critical bugs.

entries.title: How Organizations Can Use Mistakes to Level Up Their Security Programs
entries.summary: Organizations repeatedly expose ports, reuse passwords, and skip patches, creating security gaps that attackers exploit for breaches. An industry veteran outlines ways to fix these common mistakes.

entries.title: AI-Powered Dependency Decisions Introduce, Ignore Security Bugs
entries.summary: AI models often hallucinate or make costly mistakes when tasked with recommending software versions, upgrade paths, and security fixes — leading to significant technical debt.

entries.title: Intermediaries Driving Global Spyware Market Expansion
entries.summary: Third-party resellers and brokers foil transparency efforts and allow spyware to spread despite government restrictions, a study finds.

entries.title: At RSAC, the EU Leads While US Officials Are Sidelined
entries.summary: While US government sits out this year, EU officials are on the ground in San Francisco leading the conversations on today's top cybersecurity challenges.

entries.title: Blame Game: Why Public Cyber Attribution Carries Risks
entries.summary: Publicly accusing an entity of a cyberattack could have negative consequences that organizations should consider before taking the plunge.

entries.title: Phishers Pose as Palo Alto Networks' Recruiters for Months in Job Scam
entries.summary: A series of campaigns that began in August aim to defraud job candidates, using psychological tactics and data scraped from LinkedIn profiles.

entries.title: AI Dominates RSAC Innovation Sandbox
entries.summary: Ten finalists had three minutes to make their case for being the most innovative, promising young security company of the year. Geordie AI wins the 2026 contest.

entries.title: SANS: Top 5 Most Dangerous New Attack Techniques to Watch
entries.summary: For the first time, SANS Institute's five top attack techniques all have one thing in common — AI.

entries.title: Why a 'Near-Miss' Database Is Key to Improving Information Sharing
entries.summary: Organizations disclose attack details, though information may be limited. What if they did the same with close calls?

entries.title: AI-Native Security Is a Must to Counter AI-Based Attacks
entries.summary: Attacks by artificial intelligence agents are a reality. Experts at Nvidia's GTC conference say defenders need to use the same tools to fight them off.

entries.title: Ex-NSA Directors Discuss 'Red Line' for Offensive Cyberattacks
entries.summary: Four former NSA chiefs representing a near-complete history of US Cyber Command debate the role of offensive cyber in the government at RSAC.

entries.title: Iran Hacktivists Make Noise but Have Little Impact on War
entries.summary: Iran-aligned groups are trying to make their mark in the Gulf, but the results have fallen short of remarkable.

entries.title: CSA Launches CSAI Foundation for AI Security
entries.summary: The Cloud Security Alliance creates a dedicated nonprofit to govern autonomous AI agent ecosystems through risk intelligence and certification.

entries.title: Checkmarx KICS Code Scanner Targeted in Widening Supply Chain Hit
entries.summary: TeamPCP is the likely cyber threat actor behind attacks on Trivy, Checkmarx's KICS and VS Code plug-ins, and the LiteLLM AI library — and all signs point to more attacks to come.

entries.title: How AI Coding Tools Crushed the Endpoint Security Fortress
entries.summary: Security vendors have spent years building up defenses around the endpoint, but one researcher says AI coding tools have brought the walls down.

entries.title: GitHub 'OpenClaw Deployer' Repo Delivers Trojan Instead
entries.summary: An AI-assisted campaign is spreading more than 300 poisoned packages for diverse assets ranging from developer tools to game cheats.

entries.title: How a Large Bank Uses AI Digital Twins for Threat Hunting
entries.summary: JPMorganChase uses digital fingerprints and digital twins to spot online attackers and malicious behaviors while also reducing pesky false alerts.

entries.title: Microsoft Proposes Better Identity, Guardrails for AI Agents
entries.summary: Companies need better controls to manage key threats rising from the growth of agentic AI. These new features provide a starting point.

entries.title: AI in the SOC: What Could Go Wrong?
entries.summary: Two cybersecurity leaders tested out AI in their respective SOCs for six months — and here's what they learned.

entries.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Targets CI/CD Secrets
entries.summary: A threat actor used the open source security tool to deploy an infostealer into CI/CD workflows and steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, tokens, and other sensitive secrets.

entries.title: Ransomware's New Era: Moving at AI Speed
entries.summary: Threat actors bypass security tools and use AI to launch faster ransomware attacks that exploit valid credentials and target data.

entries.title: CISOs Debate Human Role in AI-Powered Security
entries.summary: The idea of a "human in the loop" in AI deployment was challenged during a security executive panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference this week.

entries.title: Attackers Hide Infostealer in Copyright Infringement Notices
entries.summary: A phishing campaign targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in various countries uses several evasion techniques to avoid detection.

entries.title: Patch Now: Oracle's Fusion Middleware Has Critical RCE Flaw
entries.summary: Attackers can execute arbitrary code without authentication if Oracle's Identity or Web Services Managers are exposed to the Web.

entries.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
entries.summary: Files on a central cloud server used by the ransomware group highlight a systematic, aggressive attack on network backups as a key TTP.

entries.title: Interlock Ransomware Targets Cisco Enterprise Firewalls
entries.summary: The ransomware gang, known for double-extortion attacks, had access to a critical Cisco firewall vulnerability weeks before it was publicly disclosed.
